name: Honey-Almond Rugelach
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl with an electric mixer on medium speed, beat the butter and cream cheese until fluffy.
2. Add 3 tablespoons honey and mix well.
3. Mix in the flour until the dough holds together.
4. Form the dough into a ball; wrap in plastic wrap and refrigerate for 2 hours.
5. Preheat oven to 350 degrees. Line cookie sheets with parchment paper and grease the parchment.
6. Divide the dough into fourths. On a lightly floured surface, roll out one piece at a time into a 9-inch circle using a rolling pin.
7. In a small bowl or cup, combine 2 tablespoons honey and lemon juice; mix well.
8. Brush the dough with some of the honey mixture and sprinkle 1/4 teaspoon cinnamon over the entire surface.
9. In another small bowl, combine the almonds, dried cherries, and the remaining 3 tablespoons honey; mix well.
10. Spread a quarter of the almond mixture onto the circle of dough, stopping 1/2-inch from the outer edge.
11. Cut the circle into eight wedges.
12. Starting at the wide outer edge, roll up each triangle jelly roll style toward the tip.
13. Gently bend both ends to form a crescent shape.
14. Place on prepared cookie sheets; refrigerate for 20 minutes or longer.
15. Repeat with the remaining dough, honey-lemon mixture, cinnamon, and filling.
16. Bake for 20 to 25 minutes, or until golden brown.
17. Remove from the oven and cool on wire racks.
18. Unbaked crescents may be stored in a freezer-safe container or bags and frozen for up to 3 months. Thaw before baking.
